REQ-160 IS ALLOCATION-BOUNDED, NOT LITERAL ZERO-ALLOCATION (dm-0119). The immutable copy-on-write core (Engine.tick spreads a fresh snapshot per fixed step; StateManager keeps current+previous) is the determinism/single-source-of-truth correctness contract (dm-0004) and MUST NOT be rewritten to be zero-alloc. S11.1 proves the sim is bounded (one snapshot/step) and the RENDER hot path is pool-served; only non-pool render-path steady-state allocation is a bug.

GENERATOR ENRICHMENT (S11.4, dm-0118) EXTENDS THE VERIFIED src/gen/ SUBTREE ADDITIVELY -- IT DOES NOT REOPEN P7. manufactureLevel's public seam signature must stay BYTE-STABLE; the seven PipelinePhase members and pipeline control flow are closed (dm-0064) and unchanged; only the template PALETTE grows. Every new template's output MUST pass the UNCHANGED Solvability/Softlock/evaluateLevel gates -- the dm-0115 softlock failure mode (four approach-modifying mechanics softlock one-jump corridors) is the thing to provably avoid, and those existing gates are the proof mechanism, not new code. All P7 tests must re-run green as an acceptance gate.

THE BUILD PIPELINE IS A ZERO-DEPENDENCY HAND-ROLLED BUNDLER (dm-0120). Do NOT add esbuild/vite/rollup or ANY npm dependency (.npmrc still pins registry.npmjs.org, private mirror still broken). tools/build/ = bundle.js (ESM-graph resolver+concatenator+minifier over dist/) + emitHtml.js (index.html) + optimizeData.js (JSON minify). The Poki SDK is CDN-referenced via a <script> tag in index.html, NEVER bundled (PortalSdk.ts: Poki server-pushes SDK updates, 'bundling is not the supported path').

render/platform/ DOES NOT EXIST YET (dm-0103 deferred it to P11; dm-0121 builds it at S11.2). It is the SOLE subtree permitted to name browser globals (window/document/requestAnimationFrame/fetch/WebGL/Canvas/PokiSDK) -- scan-enforced, both directions (nothing outside names globals; nothing inside contains logic). Its verification is a DOCUMENTED MANUAL browser-open checkpoint recorded in the S11.7 report, NOT a Playwright dependency.

SPIRAL-OF-DEATH IS ALREADY HANDLED (dm-0124): Clock.advance clamps realDeltaSeconds to MAX_FRAME_SECONDS = 0.25 (<=15 catch-up steps/frame). The rAF FrameScheduler binding must feed TIMESTAMP DELTAS (successive rAF timestamps subtracted), not an accumulated/re-derived clock. Do NOT add a second delta-clamp in the binding -- that duplicates a tested core guarantee.

REQ-005/084/015 FLIP TO VERIFIED ONLY IF THE ENRICHED-GENERATOR CAMPAIGN GENUINELY PASSES at restored (content-calibration-v2, toward DEFAULT_PROFILE) thresholds -- never by lowering the bar again. The threshold restoration is a ledgered CDRE step, distribution-level, never a per-level override (the DoD's explicit prohibition).

LEDGER APPENDS MUST GO THROUGH THE CANONICAL SERIALIZER, NOT raw json.dump. GdosDesignMemory.test.ts enforces byte-idempotency (serialize(parse(text)) === text). Use dist/src/eval/gdos/DesignMemory.js's appendDecision + serializeDesignMemory (the serializer is JSON.stringify(canonical, null, 2) + a trailing newline). A raw json.dump drops the trailing newline and fails the test.
